checks.a_analysis: The output JSON faithfully contains the body text, headings, and lists found in the source PDF. No fabricated introductory or concluding text was added, and the subject/date information matches the source exactly. All paragraphs present in the JSON can be traced directly to the source document.
No URLs, email addresses, or phone numbers were fabricated. While several URLs and links present in the source are missing from the output, no new or incorrect contact information was added.

All main sections of the memorandum (Header, Intro, State CIO Obligations, Public Agency Requirements, Recommendations, Deletion Directive, and Prohibiting Access Directive) are represented in the JSON output. Both pages of the source are accounted for.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The conversion pipeline failed to extract text associated with hyperlinks, resulting in nonsensical sentence fragments and the loss of critical directive information. Specifically, the following text is missing: 'SL 2024-26, Section 7' (Section 1 intro), 'it.nc.gov/805' (submission URL), and 'Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)' (reference policy name). Additionally, standalone link text 'it.nc.gov' appearing in headers/footers is omitted. The missing submission URL is a critical failure as it is the only location provided for mandatory reporting requirements described in the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: outlines key requirements under SL 2024-26, Section 7, and provides... via the N.C. Department of Information Technology website (it.nc.gov/805): ...refer to the Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) published by NCDIT
checks.b_evidence_output: outlines key requirements under , and provides... via the N.C. Department of Information Technology website ( ): ...refer to the  published by NCDIT

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Aside from the major omissions categorized under C5, the remaining text accurately reflects the source document's content, including names, dates, and statute references (e.g., N.C.G.S. 143-805(d)). Wording is preserved correctly in body paragraphs and lists.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output JSON against the source PDF and found no instances of duplicated paragraphs, sections, or list items. All unique text blocks in the source are represented only once in the output.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent across the document sections. On page 1, major section headings ('State CIO Reporting Obligation' and 'Public Agency Reporting Requirements') are correctly nested as H3 under the document header 'Memorandum' (H2). However, on page 2, sections of the same functional level ('Recommendations on Appropriate Viewing', 'Directive for Deleting...', and 'Directive for Prohibiting...') are promoted to H2. This creates a flat hierarchy where the page 2 sections appear as peer documents to 'Memorandum' rather than sub-sections within it.
checks.b_evidence_source: State CIO Reporting Obligation; Public Agency Reporting Requirements; Recommendations on Appropriate Viewing
checks.b_evidence_output: State CIO Reporting Obligation (level 3); Public Agency Reporting Requirements (level 3); Recommendations on Appropriate Viewing (level 2)

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no tables; therefore, no table structural defects can occur.

The reading order generally follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the document. While some elements like the logo have been moved to the end of the first section for better structural grouping, the logical progression of information remains intact. Minor gaps in the reading order sequence correspond to missing link content rather than reordered sections.

All four lists (4 items, 2 items, 3 items on page 1, and 7 items on page 2) are correctly identified as unordered lists with the correct number of list items. The nesting and item content (excluding missing links) are accurate.

Multiple functional links and their associated anchor text are entirely missing from the output. In the paragraphs where these links appear in the source, the output text is split into two separate elements with a gap between them in the reading order, omitting the text entirely. Missing links include the statutory reference 'SL 2024-26, Section 7', the URL 'it.nc.gov/805', and the policy reference 'Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)'.
checks.b_evidence_source: ...under SL 2024-26, Section 7, and...; ...website (it.nc.gov/805):; ...refer to the Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) published...
checks.b_evidence_output: ...under ' [gap] ', and provides...; ...website ( [gap] ):; ...refer to the [gap] published by...
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 1 intro paragraph; Page 1 Public Agency Reporting section; Page 2 Directive for Prohibiting section

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output exhibits several major rendering and layout failures. First, the NCDIT branding logo, which serves as a header element in the source PDF, has been displaced and rendered in the middle of the document text at the transition point between the original page 1 and page 2 content. Second, there is a significant rendering failure where hyperlinked text ('it.nc.gov/805' and 'Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)') is missing from the output, leaving empty parentheses and broken sentences in the body text. Finally, markdown formatting markers (double asterisks for bolding) are incorrectly rendered as literal text characters throughout the document, affecting professional appearance and readability.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF Page 1 header has NCDIT logo; Page 1 body: '...Technology website (it.nc.gov/805):'; Page 2 body: '...refer to the Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) published by...'.
checks.b_evidence_output: HTML logo appears after 'Public Agency Reporting Requirements'; body text shows '...website ( ):' and '...refer to the published by...'; literal '**' characters surround bolded text like '**Josh Stein**'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Document header, 'Public Agency Reporting Requirements' section, and 'Directive for Prohibiting Access' section.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table rendering issues were observed in the output.
The meaningful image from the source (the NCDIT logo) is present in the HTML output. While its placement is incorrect (as noted in Check V1), the image itself is rendered correctly and not missing.

No body content, such as paragraphs or list items, is duplicated in the output. Section headings appearing in both the Table of Contents and the body content is an intentional accessible design feature and does not constitute a defect.
